entities.name: SEMI-ARID TROPICS
entities.description: Semi-arid tropical regions characterized by specific climatic conditions

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: The country where the study is focused

entities.name: MAHABUBNAGAR DISTRICT
entities.description: A specific district in Andhra Pradesh used as a case study

entities.name: AGRICULTURE
entities.description: The practice of farming and cultivation

entities.name: FARMER INVESTMENT
entities.description: The amount of capital or resources farmers put into their agricultural activities

entities.name: AGRICULTURAL INPUTS
entities.description: Resources used in agricultural production, such as seeds and fertilizers

entities.name: FERTILIZERS
entities.description: Chemical or natural substances added to soil to increase its fertility

entities.name: PESTICIDES
entities.description: Substances used for destroying insects or other organisms harmful to cultivated plants

entities.name: SEEDS
entities.description: The basic input for planting crops

entities.name: LABOR INPUTS
entities.description: The human effort applied to agricultural production

entities.name: LAND IMPROVEMENTS
entities.description: Modifications made to land to increase its productivity

entities.name: IRRIGATION
entities.description: The artificial application of water to land or soil

entities.name: RISK
entities.description: The possibility of loss or failure in agricultural activities

entities.name: RISK AVERSION
entities.description: The tendency of farmers to avoid risk

entities.name: LOW PROFITABILITY
entities.description: A state where expected returns are low

entities.name: EXPECTED RETURNS
entities.description: The anticipated profit from agricultural investments

entities.name: CREDIT CONSTRAINTS
entities.description: Limitations on the ability of farmers to borrow money

entities.name: UNDERINVESTMENT
entities.description: A situation where investment levels are lower than optimal
